highlights: Zero TypeScript errors — strict mode is fully enforced
Zero 'any' types in src/ — excellent type discipline
Zero React patterns — pure SolidJS throughout
44 test files covering stores, services, hooks, and components
Error boundaries in place at app root and panel level
rust-bridge.ts is well-typed with proper IPC layer abstraction
concerns: 106 solid/reactivity warnings across 68 files — systemic pattern of passing reactive props directly to native event handlers
5 solid/components-return-once warnings — early returns in component render break SolidJS reactivity model
3 async createEffect scopes in AppDialogs.tsx and input-dialogs.tsx — reactivity is silently lost after first await
9 THINKING-DEBUG console.warn statements left in production hooks (use-rust-agent.ts, useAgent.ts)
tauri-agent.ts is effectively dead code — none of its exports are imported anywhere in src/
tauri-agent.ts duplicates AgentSession and ToolInfo interfaces already defined in rust-ipc.ts
AppearanceTab.tsx uses Array#map in JSX instead of <For /> (the one lint error)
No aria-label on the bulk of chat components — only layout and modal components have accessibility attributes

findings.issue: Dead module — none of its exports (agentRun, agentStream, executeTool, listTools, listenToAgentEvents) are imported anywhere in src/. AgentSession and ToolInfo are re-declared here but already exist in rust-ipc.ts as RustSession and RustToolInfo.
findings.recommendation: Delete tauri-agent.ts. All consumers use rust-bridge.ts / use-rust-agent.ts. If the type aliases are needed, re-export them from rust-ipc.ts.

findings.issue: 9 console.warn('[THINKING-DEBUG]') statements left in production code paths (handleAgentEvent, attachListener, and useAgent.ts). These fire on every agent event, polluting the console in production builds.
findings.recommendation: Replace all '[THINKING-DEBUG]' console.warn calls with log.debug() or remove them. Search for the string 'THINKING-DEBUG' across use-rust-agent.ts and useAgent.ts.

findings.issue: Async function passed directly as a tracked JSX prop (onSave): `onSave={async (desc) => { const id = await createCheckpoint(desc); ... }}`. SolidJS reactivity tracking stops at the first await — any reactive reads after the await are untracked and silent.
findings.recommendation: Extract the async logic outside of reactive tracking: use a non-reactive callback or wrap side-effects with untrack(). Pattern repeated in src/components/chat/message-input/input-dialogs.tsx lines 69 and 73.

findings.issue: Array#map() used inside JSX instead of <For />. This causes DOM elements to be recreated on every re-render rather than diffed. This is the single lint error (solid/prefer-for).
findings.recommendation: Replace `{thinkingOptions.map((opt) => ...)}` with `<For each={thinkingOptions}>{(opt) => ...}</For>`.

findings.issue: StatusIcon sub-component uses an early return (switch) before reading props.status inside JSX. Three separate solid/components-return-once warnings. SolidJS components run once — early returns mean reactive updates to props.status after mount are silently ignored.
findings.recommendation: Replace the switch statement with a `<Switch><Match when=...>` pattern or use an accessor/createMemo inside JSX.

findings.issue: Early conditional return `if (isDelegationTool(props.toolCall.name)) return <SubagentCard />` — reads props.toolCall.name outside a tracked scope. If the prop changes after mount the conditional will never re-evaluate.
findings.recommendation: Use `<Show when={isDelegationTool(props.toolCall.name)} fallback={...}>` with both branches in JSX.

findings.issue: Early return `if (structuredData() !== null) return (...)` — solid/components-return-once warning. The ToolCallOutput component won't react to subsequent changes in structuredData() after initial render.
findings.recommendation: Use `<Show when={structuredData() !== null} fallback={<DefaultOutput />}><StructuredOutputView /></Show>` instead of early return.

findings.issue: solid/reactivity warning on line 103: the callback passed to dialogs.switchRightPanelTab reads rightPanelVisible() but is not inside a tracked scope. The reactive read will be stale after first call.
findings.recommendation: Wrap the callback body with createMemo or move the reactive read to a getter that is properly tracked.

findings.issue: solid/reactivity warning on line 160: the handler closure inside setupShortcutListener reads getResolvedShortcuts() (a reactive accessor) but the closure is not inside a tracked scope — it's passed to document.addEventListener. Updates to shortcuts after the listener is set up won't be reflected.
findings.recommendation: Inside the handler, call getResolvedShortcuts() to get a fresh snapshot at event time — this already reads reactively on call. Alternatively, materialise the shortcuts list with createMemo outside the listener and close over the memo result.

findings.issue: Three solid/reactivity warnings: async functions passed as callbacks to runGitAction read reactive values (branch(), projectDir()) after an await boundary. Reactivity tracking is lost mid-function.
findings.recommendation: Capture reactive values into local variables synchronously before the await: `const dir = projectDir(); const br = branch();` then use those locals inside the async callback.

findings.issue: Two solid/reactivity warnings: props.error.message and props.error.stack are read in the top-level of the ErrorFallback component body (in a log call) outside a tracked scope. The eslint-disable comment acknowledges this is intentional ('one-time log at mount'), but the reactive reads are still flagged.
findings.recommendation: The existing eslint-disable comment is correct. However, consider using `untrack(() => props.error.message)` to make the intent explicit and silence the warning correctly.

findings.issue: AgentSession interface declares `messages: unknown[]` — overly loose typing. RustSession in rust-ipc.ts types this as `Record<string, JsonValue>[]` which is more precise.
findings.recommendation: Since tauri-agent.ts is dead code (see critical finding), delete the file. If interfaces must remain, align with rust-ipc.ts definitions.

findings.issue: solid/reactivity: `props.provider.apiKey` and `props.provider.id` are read in the component setup body (line 42 and 46) outside JSX. If the parent updates the provider prop, these reads won't re-run.
findings.recommendation: Move reactive reads to JSX or wrap in createMemo/createEffect. For the initial signal value `createSignal(props.provider.apiKey ? '...' : '')`, this is intentional one-time init — mark explicitly with a comment.

findings.issue: Three solid/style-prop warnings: string-style props on <span> elements (`style="animation-delay: 0ms"`). SolidJS requires style as an object for proper SSR and reactive updates.
findings.recommendation: Replace `style="animation-delay: 0ms"` with `style={{ 'animation-delay': '0ms' }}` for all three splash-dot spans.

findings.issue: Duplicate invoke() helper defined locally. rust-bridge.ts already provides an invokeCommand() abstraction with proper error wrapping. This local version bypasses the error-message normalization and [rust-bridge:cmd] prefix logging.
findings.recommendation: Import and use invokeCommand from rust-bridge.ts rather than defining a second Tauri/HTTP dispatch function.

findings.issue: Zero aria-label/role attributes on chat UI components (MessageBubble, ToolCallCard, ApprovalDock, ChatView, MessageInput). Only layout and modal components have accessibility attributes. Icon-only buttons lack accessible names.
findings.recommendation: Add aria-label to all icon-only buttons. Use role='status' or aria-live='polite' for streaming content and tool status updates. Minimum: label the Cancel/Approve buttons in ApprovalDock and the Send button in message-input.

findings.issue: Dead code block: lines 141-143 `if (editing() === 'new' || editing()) { // This is reactive — handled below in JSX }` — the if body is empty. The condition reads a reactive accessor for no effect.
findings.recommendation: Remove lines 141-143 entirely. The Show component below already handles this reactively.

findings.issue: Two async handlers passed as JSX props (onApplySelected, onApplyAll) on SandboxReviewDialog. These are tracked scopes that go async, losing reactivity after the first await.
findings.recommendation: Extract the async logic to named functions defined outside JSX. Pass the function reference rather than an inline async arrow.

findings.issue: The 106 solid/reactivity warnings (props.onXxx passed directly as event handler binding) are a systemic pattern across 68 files. The pattern `onClick={props.onClose}` is flagged as potentially non-reactive if the parent changes the prop after mount.
findings.recommendation: Use `onClick={() => props.onClose()}` consistently for all prop-forwarded event handlers. Many components already do this correctly — apply the same pattern globally. The ESLint --fix flag resolves 3 of these automatically.

findings.issue: Only 44 test files exist for a frontend with 68+ component files and 20+ service/store files. Critical paths like ChatView, MessageInput, the agent bridge (use-rust-agent.ts), and store mutations lack component-level tests.
findings.recommendation: Add integration tests for: (1) useRustAgent hook event handling, (2) settings store mutations (settings-mutators.ts), (3) the full MessageInput submit flow, (4) the AppShell layout state transitions.

findings.issue: No test coverage for the settings persistence/sync layer despite it being a critical path (user config loss on failure would be high-impact).
findings.recommendation: Add tests for settings-mutators.ts covering provider updates, model switching, and MCP config changes with mock Tauri IPC.

findings.issue: Stores use module-level createSignal (singleton pattern) rather than SolidJS createStore. This means derived values require manual memoization and there is no built-in fine-grained reactivity for nested objects.
findings.recommendation: Consider migrating complex stores (settings, session) to createStore for automatic nested reactivity. The layout store's flat signals pattern is fine and should stay as-is.

coverage_gaps: useChat.ts (chat orchestration, integration test exists but limited)

type_safety.notable: rust-ipc.ts defines JsonValue as a recursive type union — clean approach for IPC boundary. tauri-agent.ts uses `messages: unknown[]` (dead code). Record<string, unknown> appears in IPC invoke signatures which is appropriate.
